page_content:
CHEM612 Scientific Presentations (1 Credit)

Workshops will cover all aspects of giving scientific presentations. Each student will give a presentation based oon the topic of his/her final paper in CHEM611. Presentations will be critiqued by peers and faculty members.

Prerequisite: CHEM611.

CHEM623 Optical Methods of Quantitative Analysis (3 Credits)

The quantitative applications of various methods of optical spectroscopy. Prerequisite: CHEM482; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department.

CHEM624 Electrical Methods of Quantitative Analysis (3 Credits) The use of conductivity, potentiometry, polarography, voltammetry, amperometry, coulometry, and chronopotentiometry in quantitative analysis.

Prerequisite: CHEM482; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department.
